# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 581
- **Lines of code**: 168,710
- **Languages**: TypeScript (473), CSS (27), JSON (26), Other (20), JavaScript (17)
- **Zones**: 7 (7 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/from-recommend @ a67e98f
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-11T17:45:43.467Z

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/login → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_auth.login.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Test coupling to implementation: packages-sourcevision test suite has direct calls to analysis engine internals rather than testing through public interface [critical]
- Zone size imbalance: 36x difference between largest (144 files) and smallest (4 files) zones indicates uneven responsibility distribution [info]
- Fan-in concentration risk: 3 modules (tree.ts, routes-rex.ts, CallGraphView) handle disproportionate call volume, creating single points of failure for maintenance [warning]
- God function: CallGraphView in packages/web/src/viewer/views/call-graph.ts calls 67 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Four-tier layered architecture with unidirectional flow: orchestration → execution → domain → foundation — no layer bypassing or circular dependencies across 1403 import edges [warning]
- Critical architectural debt concentration: 3 god-level components across 2 zones handle 60% of system complexity - tree.ts (31 fan-in), routes-rex.ts (129 calls), CallGraphView (67 function calls) [critical]
- Systematic dead export accumulation across 4+ zones suggests missing cleanup automation — consider adding unused export detection to CI pipeline [critical]

### Relationships

- Interface-based zone coupling: dashboard-platform's 21 outgoing calls represent clean service composition rather than tight coupling — dependency injection through gateway modules [critical]
- Dead export accumulation pattern: 15+ unused exports across 4 zones suggests missing cleanup automation - implement pre-commit hook using ts-prune or similar to prevent export debt [info]
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 129 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [warning]

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-client)
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 144 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Architecture successfully implements zero-circular-dependency design with clear tier separation from orchestration down to foundation [critical]
- Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts receives calls from 31 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects [warning]
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (root)
- Contains 45% of project files (144/322) — subdivided into 3 sub-zones [info] (web)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (web)

### Suggestions

- Establish consistent import extension strategy across all zones: either use .js extensions everywhere (Node.js ESM) or .ts extensions (TypeScript-style) [info]
- Implement fan-in monitoring: add CI check to flag modules with >20 incoming calls or functions with >30 outgoing calls to prevent future god-component formation [warning]
- Hub function: walkTree in packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts is called from 22 files — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [warning]
- 6 potentially unused exports in packages/web/src/schema/validate.ts have no incoming calls: validateManifest, validateInventory, validateImports, validateZones, validateComponents (+1 more) [warning]
- 3 potentially unused exports in packages/web/src/viewer/components/data-display/health-gauge.ts have no incoming calls: HealthGauge, PatternBadge, MetricCard [info]
- 3 potentially unused exports in packages/web/src/viewer/components/logos.ts have no incoming calls: NdxLogoPng, ProductLogoPng, BrandedHeader [info]
- 2 potentially unused exports in packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts have no incoming calls: loadSuggestionHistoryAsync, saveSuggestionHistoryAsync [info]
- Potentially unused export: cmdConfig in packages/hench/src/cli/commands/config.ts has no incoming calls — verify if still needed [info]
- Validate tree.ts centralization: audit the 31 calling files to ensure they use walkTree appropriately and aren't bypassing the abstraction with direct tree manipulation [warning] (rex)
- Decompose CallGraphView god function: extract rendering logic to packages/web/src/viewer/components/graph-renderer.ts, data processing to packages/web/src/viewer/utils/graph-processor.ts, and event handling to packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/useGraphEvents.ts [critical] (web)
- ... and 2 more
